stage-1-film-filters: Stage 1 loads the diffusion model and applies LoRA modifiers, acting like the film emulsion you've chosen (e.g. Kodak Portra 400) and any filters you place in front of the lens. LoRAs adjust the model's style — Detail controls just that, the volume of fine details in the image (not sharpess) and uses a scale from 0-10. Chiaroscuro shifts lighting contrast on a scale from 0-1.0 (higher values create more dramatic lighting). These filters shape every image produced downstream.
stage-2-subject-style: In Stage 2, Prompt describes the subject, style, lighting, and other details about the image in natural prose. Film Format chooses the aspect ratio from among classic medium format still photo shapes. Images are landscape orientation by default, check the Portrait box to create a vertical image. The Prompt is encoded into CLIP embeddings that guide the diffusion process while Film Format sets the latent image dimensions.
stage-3-develop-contact: Stage 3 runs a relatively quick 6-step diffusion pass to produce a Contact Print — a fast preview for evaluating composition and prompt adherence before committing to further refinement stages.
stage-4-work-print: Stage 4 is like making a modestly-enlarged Work Print. It performs a latent-space upscale of the Contact Print (default 1.5×) and runs a second 4-step diffusion pass, then a sharpening pass, adding detail and resolution without changing the image too much. Earlier stages are cached so only this new diffusion pass is computed.
stage-5-final-print: Stage 5 is like making a fine gallery print. It performs an AI upscale using SeedVR2 (7B), using the same scaling factor as Stage 4, then adds final sharpening. This produces the highest-resolution output. You can optionally skip Stage 4/Work Print and make the Fine Print directly from the Contact Print.
